response: Japan is home to a population of 125 million and boasts the world's third-largest economy, with a GDP of $4.2 trillion growing at a modest rate of 1.1%. The country maintains a remarkably low unemployment rate of 2.6%, though it grapples with 3.2% inflation and a staggering debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%. Despite these fiscal challenges, Japan continues to perform strongly on the global stage with $756 billion in exports and enjoys one of the world's highest life expectancies at 84.6 years.
